授業方法: Lecture and discussion of reading assignments.

第1回:
【Start-up Week】
Online class (On-demand format) / 《30 minutes》
Orientation, Overview of the Course
第2回:
【Core Weeks ①】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Introduction to Premodern Japanese Literature
第3回:
【Core Weeks ②】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Myths and Legends: Kojiki
第4回:
【Core Weeks ③】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Heian Literature I: Ki no Tsurayuki and the Development of Kana Literature (Tosa Diary & Kokinshū)
第5回:
【Core Weeks ④】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Heian Literature II: The World of Sei Shōnagon (The Pillow Book)
第6回:
【Core Weeks ⑤】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Heian Literature III: The Sarashina Diary
第7回:
【Core Weeks ⑥】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Medieval Literature I: Tales of the Heike
第8回:
【Core Weeks ⑦】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Medieval Literature II: The Yoshitsune Chronicle
第9回:
【Core Weeks ⑧】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Medieval Literature III: Tale of the Soga Brothers
第10回:
【Core Weeks ⑨】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Edo Literature I: Matsuo Basho (The Narrow Road to Oku)
第11回:
【Core Weeks ⑩】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Edo Literature II: Chikamatsu Monzaemon
第12回:
【Core Weeks ⑪】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
Edo Literature III: Ueda Akinari
第13回:
【Core Weeks ⑫】
Face-to-face class / 《90 minutes》
 Workshop: Bookmaking in Old Japan
In this class, we will discuss the books themselves, rather than their contents. Students will try their hand at putting together a book in one of the traditional styles.
トピック.授業計画1:
【Flex Hours】
Online class（Task-oriented format） / 《240 minutes》
Students will be offered a menu of activities from which to fulfill these flex hours.  These activities will provide students with an opportunity to experience traditions, places, and events related to the literature that we study in class.
トピック.授業外学習2: Every week, we will discuss Japanese literature using English translations of important texts. There will be reading and visual materials for the topic provided by the instructor through LUNA. Students will be expected to read these materials before class and be prepared to actively discuss them.

参考書/Reference books1: Seeds in the Heart: Japanese Literature from Earliest Times to the Late Sixteenth Century
参考書/Reference books3: おくのほそ道 (Oku no Hosomichi)
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: There will be a quiz in class each week based on the previous week's reading and lecture.
成績評価Grading2: Students will write an essay at the end of the semester about a text or topic that interests them. Grades will be based on: originality, logical structure, effective use of quotations from a work of literature, proper use of citation and reference materials.
成績評価Grading3: 授業外学修課題／Assignment, homework required outside class (06)
Students will post questions and comments on LUNA for each week's readings.
成績評価Grading4: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)

Throughout the semester, students will research characters, places, or concepts that intrigue them in the readings. Students will take turns helping to lead in-class discussions by making short presentations about their research findings in class.

name: 【300】総合日本学習科目（日本文学Ｂ)／JSC Japanese literature B
担当者: マッケンジー　コーリ（MCKENZIE CORY）
